free_text: - data for Pb-206
Fast-neutron scattering from nuclides in the lead
region
Neutrons were produced by the T(p,n) and D(d,n)
reactions using gas-targets.
The lead and bismuth scattering samples were
cylindrical, 1.6 cm in diameter, by 1.9 cm long, with
an axial hole 0.15 cm in diameter so that they could be
strung on a movable wire for easy handling. Each
weighed about 50 g.
Plastic scintillator of 5 in. diam. coupled to
photomultiplier.
Corrected for isotopic purity of the lead samples and
for attenuation by the samples.
Corrections for multiple scattering were made only for
the elastic scattering data; for inelastic scattering
they are assumed to be small because of the
near-isotropy of the results.
Digitizing error
Digitizing error
Data on figures are given in Fermi**2/steradian,
compiler multiplied data by ten to get units: mb/sr.
SD: BIB update. Data were redigitized.
A few datasets were added. Subent 5 was added.
Sample enriched to more than 99% 206Pb.
The errors include an estimate of the
uncertainties associated with each point arising from
the problems of subtracting proper backgrounds and
resolving closely spaced states.
In addition to the errors shown on each
point, an estimated uncertainty of 5 to 7% is to be
associated with the normalization for the points
associated with a given state of excitation.
Digitized data from fig.8a,b in PR,159,969,1967

The relative errors associated with each
point are estimated to lie within the diameter of each
point shown on fig.11, except first point
The error in absolute values is estimated
be in the range of +-3%.
These data have been corrected for multiple scattering
by Monte Carlo methods
